title: Detect TTY vs pipe before formatting
description: Check whether stdout/stdin is a terminal and disable color, spinners, and interactive prompts when output is piped or redirected.
body: Use when a CLI uses color, progress, or prompts. Do not unconditionally emit ANSI color or block on a prompt, and do not assume a human is watching. Actions: test isatty on the relevant stream; enable color/spinners/prompts only on a TTY, fall back to plain output and sensible defaults (or fail with a clear message) when piped, and honor NO_COLOR and a --no-color flag. Validation signals include piped output containing no escape codes and the tool not hanging for input when stdin is not a terminal. Risk boundary: a prompt on a non-interactive stdin hangs scripts and CI forever — never block on input without a TTY check or an explicit non-interactive mode.
